frame_third_condition.note: the public milestone is a CONJUNCTION of three conditions; the launch-sigma legs (#217/#222/#229/#218/#228) price only TPS/lambda. The third condition PPL<=2.42 is UNPRICED as a function of the build's acceptance aggressiveness. Frontier #52 serves 2.3772 -> margin 0.0428 (1.77%). The int4 verify uses 3.14% of the 5% PPL budget (reference 2.30476 = cap/1.05).

handoff_lines.fern_231: the public milestone's third condition PPL<=2.42 has headroom 0.0428 at the lambda=0.9780 build bar (drift tolerance +0.3234 in divergent-accept-fraction from #52's 2.3772 anchor under the 56% int4 divergence), and it is LAMBDA-INVARIANT (output-equivalence: the served stream is the int4 greedy stream, so the build's acceptance aggressiveness cannot drift PPL) -- so PPL is NOT a binding public gate on the launch-sigma axis; land #71's served run reports the scorer's PPL natively, so it is one-run-confirmable but has comfortable slack (a verify-model change, not lambda, is the only thing that moves it); fern #231 carries ppl_headroom_at_build_bar=0.0428 as the third public-gate row alongside the TPS>=500 trigger.

decoupling.note: across the lambda grid the public speed climbs 8.2% (481.53->520.95 TPS) while ppl_served stays pinned at 2.3772 (headroom 0.0428) -- PPL is DECOUPLED from the build's acceptance axis.

lambda_projection.land71_readout_flag: land #71's served run reports the scorer's PPL natively (vLLM prompt_logprobs path, program.md submission contract) -> PPL is a ONE-RUN-CONFIRMABLE readout in the GO-card, not a pre-launch unknown. It is NOT free: it must be READ from the served run, but it is pinned at the int4 verify's 2.3772 and cannot drift with lambda.

mixture_calibration.note: mixture calibrated to #52: agreeing tokens ~ reference 2.30476 (f_div=0), divergent tokens ~ 2.4355 (the f_div=1 sub-PPL), operating f_div=0.5608 round-trips served 2.377200 (resid 4.44e-16). The divergent SUB-component 2.4355 sits ABOVE the cap, but it is only 56.1% of the stream -> the served AVERAGE is 2.3772. The served average would cross 2.42 only at f_div*=0.8841 -- a +0.3234 increase the build CANNOT make via lambda (output-equivalence).

honest_bookend.note: the served stream IS the int4-standalone greedy stream (output-equivalence), so the worst the served AVERAGE can reach is the int4-standalone PPL 2.3772 itself -- which is 0.0428 UNDER the cap. The divergent-token sub-PPL 2.4355 is higher (above cap) but is only a 56.1% sub-fraction, NOT the served average.

robustness.publish_first_is_launch_gate: under #124 publish-first, PPL<=2.42 is a condition of the PUBLIC milestone itself -- a LAUNCH gate read at submission, not a post-hoc private-bar defence. A PPL breach would FAIL the public milestone directly even with TPS>=500 and 128/128. That makes pricing it (this leg) load-bearing -- and the result is that the int4 verify keeps it pinned 0.0428 under cap, lambda-invariant.

synthesis.verdict: NOT-BINDING (on the launch-sigma lambda/tree axis). The public milestone's third condition PPL<=2.42 has headroom 0.0428 at the lambda=0.9780 build bar -- and that headroom is LAMBDA-INVARIANT. program.md L27-28 pins greedy identity to the SUBMITTED (int4) checkpoint, so the served stream IS the int4-standalone greedy stream and a correct speculative/tree decode changes only THROUGHPUT, never the served-token distribution. Hence ppl_served(lambda) = int4_standalone_ppl = 2.3772 for ALL lambda (d(ppl)/d(lambda)=0); the divergent-accept fraction is the int4-vs-target rate 0.5608 on the served=int4 stream and is ITSELF lambda-invariant. The mixture calibrates exactly (resid 4.4e-16): agreeing tokens ~ reference 2.3048, divergent sub-PPL 2.4355 (above cap but only 56% of the stream), served AVERAGE 2.3772. The served average would cross 2.42 only at f_div*=0.8841 -- a +0.3234 increase the build CANNOT make via lambda (only a coarser VERIFY MODEL could). Robust across the divergence sweep [0.50,0.60] (served PPL is the measured anchor, divergence-invariant; f_div margin stays positive). ppl_is_binding_public_gate=False. The honest bookend: the worst the served average can reach by adding divergence is the int4-standalone 2.3772 itself (0.0428 under cap). land #71's served run reports the scorer's PPL natively -> one-run-confirmable, comfortable slack. BASELINE 481.53 untouched. NOT a launch.
